edges.notes: Ditransitive extends transitive by adding a Recipient argument; encodes intended or actual transfer (Goldberg 1995)
Caused motion extends transitive by adding a directional PP; encodes the Theme's change of location as constructionally entailed (Goldberg 1995)
Resultative extends transitive by adding a result XP encoding the end-state of the object (Goldberg 1995)
Passive shares the semantics of transitive but differs in information structure: the undergoer is topicalized and the actor is demoted or omitted (Goldberg 2005)
Way construction is a subtype of caused motion in which Agent and Theme are coreferential; encodes self-propelled motion to a goal by means of the action denoted by V (Goldberg 1995)
The result XP of the resultative can be interpreted as a metaphorical Goal via the CHANGE IS MOTION conceptual metaphor, linking resultative to caused motion (Goldberg 1995: 115)
Time-away shares the NP V NP.poss X structure with the way construction but encodes temporal expenditure rather than spatial motion (Jackendoff 1997)
